message:
perf(storage): cache batch-insert SQL and store null/msgpack-only extras as SQL NULL

Two related changes to the franken-backed message-insert path that
share the same cluster of touched lines.

1) Cache the per-row-count batch-insert SQL string.
   franken_batch_insert_new_messages_with_batch_size() and
   franken_batch_insert_new_messages_with_profile_batch_size() were
   re-building the same parameterized INSERT
   ("INSERT INTO messages(...) VALUES (?1,?2,...,?8),(?9,...) ...")
   on every chunk via format!() + a fold over a placeholder Vec.
   For a typical insert flow that processes the workload in 50- and
   100-row batches, this is the same string every iteration.

   message_insert_batch_sql(row_count) precomputes the SQL for every
   1..=max(MESSAGE_INSERT_BATCH_SIZE, APPEND_MESSAGE_INSERT_BATCH_SIZE)
   row count into a OnceLock-backed Vec<String>, indexed by row
   count. Both batch paths now look up &str instead of building a
   String — drops the format! cost from the hot loop and removes the
   allocation, and the profile-aware variant's sql_build_duration
   field correspondingly drops to near zero.

   The single-row fall-through still goes through
   franken_insert_new_message() with the static SQL it already used,
   so the cache only has to cover the batched range.

2) Store JSON null / msgpack-only extras as SQL NULL.
   franken_message_insert_payload() previously returned
   (Cow<'_, str>, Option<Vec<u8>>) — an *always-present* JSON
   string plus an optional msgpack blob. For messages whose
   extra_json is the JSON value `null`, the column was being
   written as the literal text "null", and for messages whose
   payload serialized cleanly to msgpack the JSON column was being
   written redundantly with the same data in two encodings.

   New return type is (Option<Cow<'_, str>>, Option<Vec<u8>>) with
   three-way semantics:
   - historical raw JSON → (Some(Cow::Borrowed(raw)), None)
   - extra_json is JSON null → (None, None) → SQL stores NULL/NULL
   - msgpack succeeds → (None, Some(bytes)) → SQL stores NULL/blob,
     dropping the redundant JSON encoding
   - msgpack fails → (Some(Cow::Owned(json_text)), None)

   franken_read_message_extra_compat() is the matching reader.
   Reads extra_bin first (msgpack), falls back to extra_json
   (string), falls back to JSON null. The two existing
   franken_read_metadata_compat() call sites in the message
   loaders are updated to use the new helper so reads agree with
   writes.

Two new tests cover the storage shape directly:
- message_insert_stores_null_extra_json_as_sql_null asserts both
  extra_json and extra_bin columns are SQL NULL when the source
  message's extra_json is JSON null, and that fetch_messages
  round-trips the value back to JSON null.
- message_insert_stores_nonempty_extra_json_as_msgpack_only asserts
  that an object payload writes extra_bin only (not extra_json) and
  still round-trips to the original JSON via the new compat reader.

Net effect: smaller rows on disk for the common case, fewer
allocations on the hot insert loop, and the read side stays correct
for historical rows that still carry a "null" string in extra_json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
